column3: Details
Many businesses combine sales, rentals, and services to create recurring revenue.
Online-only competitors exist, but physical showrooms still play a role in product-heavy industries.
Franchise models allow faster expansion but make standardization and compliance harder.
Franchisees often create custom workflows and product naming, leading to data inconsistencies.

Retail/Distribution: Lack of Visibility in Sales & Inventory
column3: Without a unified system, franchisors struggle to track top-selling products, supplier negotiations, and profitability.
Owners resist new systems if they believe their existing process (QuickBooks, paper records) works fine. Compliance tracking is critical.
Multi-location businesses lack real-time inventory tracking, causing stock shortages and overstocking.
Without centralized sales data, businesses miss out on bulk purchasing discounts and better supplier negotiations.
Some franchises allow location-based product selection, but this complicates supplier agreements and customer expectations.
Different locations use different payment processors, creating inconsistencies in reporting and fees.
Many small businesses rely on QuickBooks for transactions, but it lacks multi-location visibility and standardization.

Retail/Distribution: Customization is Key for Multi-Entity Businesses
column3: Unlike rigid one-size-fits-all ERPs, retailers and distributors need flexible, customizable CRMs.
Retailers must track compliance, sales trends, and inventory movement in real-time to stay competitive.
Many franchise owners prefer manual processes (paper, spreadsheets) over adopting new software.

Primary Industry: MWD: Common Pitfalls in Retail & Distribution Technology Adoption
Retail/Distribution: Lack of Process Definition Before Automation
column3: Companies often try to automate without first standardizing their processes, leading to inefficiencies.
Employees find ways to bypass workflows, such as manually entering transactions in QuickBooks instead of using a CRM.
Too much customization creates maintenance issues—there must be a balance between flexibility and standardization.
Businesses need real-time visibility into sales, inventory, and financials to make strategic decisions.
Standardized workflows improve sales tracking, inventory control, and supplier negotiations.
Many multi-entity businesses struggle with enforcing consistent processes across franchisees.

Retail/Distribution: Supply Chain Efficiency Requires Sales Insights
column3: Clear sales data enables better procurement planning and demand forecasting.
Businesses need flexibility but must enforce core processes for consistency.
